text.title: scope-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for scope-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Maintaining the RTM only at project start and never updating it during delivery.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An abandoned RTM creates a false sense of coverage; gaps accumulate invisibly and surface as acceptance failures or post-launch defects.

failure-mode.description: Locking scope baseline too early without adequate stakeholder validation, then defending it against legitimate clarifications.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Premature baseline lock traps the project in obsolete decisions; balance with disciplined change control that distinguishes clarifications (no cost/schedule impact) from scope additions (require approval).

failure-mode.description: Gold plating — developers adding unrequested features to "improve" the product.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gold plating consumes contingency, delays tested deliverables, and introduces unreviewed scope that may fail acceptance or create downstream support burden.
